title: Work Buckets (Named Changelists). Add the ability to group local changes into logical buckets so users can manage multiple streams of work before committing. This is a differentiation feature that directly supports the solo power-developer workflow.
design: This task introduces named changelists/work buckets as a higher-level organization model for local work. The aim is to let users hold multiple streams of intent without committing prematurely.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Buckets can be created and managed.
2. Changes can be associated with buckets.
3. Bucket-aware commit workflows make practical sense.
notes: This should complement staging, not replace it.

comments.author: NamPT
comments.text: Reasoning note: work buckets should help users express intent, not create a second confusing staging system. Keep the mental model crisp.

title: Bucket data model and persistence
description: Define how work buckets are represented and persisted so they survive app restarts and remain understandable over time.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Bucket data model and persistence is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-11g without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: Bucket-aware staging and commit actions
description: Connect bucket membership to staging and commit flows in a way that strengthens user intent rather than duplicating confusion.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Bucket-aware staging and commit actions is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-11g without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: Spike B: Canvas Commit Graph Engine
description: Build and validate the production baseline for mongit's Canvas 2D commit graph. This task exists because the graph is a core differentiator and a known technical risk. Scope includes graph data flow, lane assignment, renderer behavior, interaction primitives, and measurable performance evidence for large histories; it does not include advanced search or branch-collapse UX yet.
design: This task closes the highest-risk visual/technical foundation for mongit. Separate the graph into a deterministic data pipeline, a scalable renderer, and measurable interaction/performance behavior so later history features build on proven primitives rather than ad hoc rendering.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Graph layout is deterministic for real repository history.
2. Canvas rendering stays responsive on large commit sets.
3. Interaction primitives exist for selection, inspection, and future navigation features.
4. Performance evidence is captured rather than assumed.
notes: Out of scope: advanced search, collapse/expand, or history-analysis features; this task is about a production baseline.

comments.author: NamPT
comments.text: Reasoning note: the project has already chosen Canvas 2D over DOM/WebGL for pragmatic reasons. The main unresolved question is no longer “what rendering technology?” but “what proves this approach is production-worthy at the target scale?”

comments.author: NamPT
comments.text: Refined: draft → prd.md (score: 93/100, 1 round)

title: Graph data pipeline and lane algorithm
description: Create the deterministic pipeline that transforms repository commit/ref data into graph nodes, edges, and lane assignments. This subtask exists to keep layout correctness independent from drawing concerns and to give future graph features a stable semantic model.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Graph data pipeline and lane algorithm is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-12d without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Canvas renderer and viewport virtualization is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-12d without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

description: Add the interaction hooks and measurable performance checks needed to prove the graph is production-worthy. This should capture selection/inspection readiness and produce evidence future agents can compare against.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Graph interaction and performance benchmark suite is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-12d without widening scope beyond the stated description.

title: Foundation Completion: finish the remaining technical spikes and baseline platform work that unblock the rest of mongit. This epic covers the commit graph production baseline, diff/watcher foundation, design system baseline, and reliability guardrails so future work can build on stable primitives instead of spikes.
design: Purpose: close the remaining foundation spikes and stabilize the baseline architecture before broad feature expansion. This epic exists because the roadmap still lists commit-graph validation, diff/watcher integration, and design-system completion as prerequisites for confident MVP work. Future agents should treat this epic as platform work: improve primitives, prove risky assumptions, and document what good looks like for future slices.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Commit graph foundation is measurably validated on large histories.
2. Diff viewer and watcher backbone are reliable enough to power staging/conflict workflows.
3. Design tokens and base primitives are stable enough for repeated reuse.
4. Future agents can verify foundation health quickly with documented guardrails.
notes: Out of scope: full MVP polish, advanced history tooling, AI features, and platform expansion. The intent is to turn spikes into trustworthy building blocks.

comments.author: NamPT
comments.text: Background for future self: current code already has a real Tauri/Svelte/Rust base, a working graph surface, watcher plumbing, and substantial backend Git operations. This epic exists to convert those promising pieces into validated platform primitives. The strategic reason is leverage: every later slice gets cheaper and less risky if graph, diff/watcher, and design tokens are trustworthy first.

title: Commit Graph Productization
description: Convert the existing graph spike into a reliable user-facing history surface with commit details, usable ref overlays, and day-to-day navigation quality. This task should build on the graph foundation rather than re-spiking it.
design: This task converts the existing graph foundation into a daily-use history surface. The emphasis is on dependable detail presentation and navigation quality, not just rendering large graphs.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Commit selection and detail inspection feel reliable.
2. Ref overlays are usable on real histories.
3. The graph integrates cleanly into the main app shell.
notes: Build on Spike B outputs; do not re-spike the renderer.

title: Graph-detail binding in main app shell
description: Connect commit selection to a dependable detail surface inside the main app shell. This turns the graph from a visual spike into a usable navigation tool.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Graph-detail binding in main app shell is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-145 without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: Branch and tag overlays for real histories

acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Branch and tag overlays for real histories is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-145 without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: Pre-Commit Check Pipeline
description: Add configurable local quality gates that can run before finalizing a commit, with clear reporting and explicit bypass semantics. This supports disciplined history creation for power users.
design: This task adds a local quality gate pipeline before commit finalization. The intent is to support disciplined history creation without turning the app into an opaque CI system.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Ordered local checks can run before commit finalize.
2. Results are visible and actionable.
3. Bypass behavior is explicit rather than hidden.
notes: Checks should remain configurable and local-first.

title: Check runner framework

notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: Canvas 2D commit graph renderer (10k+ commits, 60fps)

close_reason: Shipped: all 18 PRD criteria met. Canvas 2D renderer handles 10k+ commits at 60fps with virtual scrolling, DAG layout, branch coloring, ref labels, and keyboard navigation.

comments.author: NamPT
comments.text: Created prd.md: score 92/100, 4 refinement rounds, 8 tasks, 7 success criteria

title: V1.0 Full Solo Workflow: expand mongit from MVP into a deep standalone Git client with advanced history investigation, richer graph ergonomics, advanced branch operations, stash/tag support, and safety-net/undo capabilities aligned with the product thesis.
design: Purpose: expand MVP into a true standalone daily-driver with JetBrains-grade depth for solo developers. This epic exists to close the gap between a practical MVP and the project thesis of deep features plus native performance.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Advanced history investigation is available in-app.
2. Advanced branch operations cover the most common professional workflows.
3. Safety and undo affordances reduce fear around risky actions.
4. Users need the terminal less often for day-to-day Git work.
notes: Out of scope: AI-first features and cross-platform expansion; those belong to later phases after workflow depth is earned.

comments.author: NamPT
comments.text: Background for future self: V1 is about earning the product thesis of JetBrains-grade depth in a standalone client. The bar is not feature count alone; it is whether advanced Git users can stay in mongit for serious daily work with less friction and less fear.

title: History Investigation Suite. Add file history, blame-driven navigation, and commit comparison workflows so mongit becomes genuinely useful for debugging and code archaeology rather than only change authoring.
design: This task makes mongit useful for investigation and archaeology, not just mutation. File history, blame navigation, and commit comparison should answer who/when/why questions in a way that reduces context switching.
acceptance_criteria:
1. File history is navigable in-app.
2. Blame connects to useful history context.
3. Commit comparison supports debugging and review.
notes: This is core to the project's JetBrains-inspired value, not an optional extra.

title: File history panel
description: Create the focused file-history surface that lets users inspect how a single file evolved over time.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: File history panel is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-1mk without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: Blame and compare navigation
description: Link blame output and commit comparison into a useful investigative flow rather than isolated features.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Blame and compare navigation is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-1mk without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: macOS Packaging and Distribution Baseline. Produce installable MVP artifacts and the release-path basics needed for external testing and early distribution. Scope includes reproducible release builds, packaging, and install/smoke documentation.
design: This task converts MVP from an internal development state into something external testers can actually install and run. Packaging is part of product reality, not just release ceremony.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Release artifacts are reproducible.
2. Installation path is documented.
3. Basic smoke testing can be performed outside the author environment.
notes: macOS-only is acceptable at this phase; do not conflate this with Windows support.

title: Release artifact pipeline
description: Make release builds reproducible and packageable so mongit can be tested outside the author machine.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Release artifact pipeline is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-1sy without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: Install documentation and smoke checklist
description: Document installation and the initial acceptance flow so external testers can validate the MVP consistently.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Install documentation and smoke checklist is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-1sy without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: V2.0 Intelligence and Expansion: extend mongit beyond parity into stacked diffs, AI-assisted workflow intelligence, Windows support, and optional cloud features, while preserving the core standalone-first philosophy and keeping assistive features additive rather than mandatory.
design: Purpose: extend mongit into next-generation workflows and broader reach without abandoning the standalone-first identity. This phase should be ambitious but additive: advanced users should gain power without core behavior becoming dependent on cloud or AI.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Stacked workflows are first-class.
2. AI assistance is bounded, optional, and trustworthy.
3. Windows support reaches practical parity for core flows.
4. Optional cloud features remain clearly opt-in.
notes: Guard the philosophy here: assistive features should complement deterministic Git operations, never replace them.

comments.author: NamPT
comments.text: Background for future self: treat this phase as strategic expansion, not license for unfocused experimentation. New intelligence and platform work should strengthen the core proposition rather than blur it.

title: Safety Net and Undo Framework. Introduce previews, recovery affordances, and rollback guidance around risky Git actions so mongit embodies the 'undo everything' principle as far as Git permits.
design: This task embodies the project principle of operation preview and undo. The goal is to make risky Git operations feel legible and recoverable enough that users trust the client with serious work.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Risky operations have previews or explicit recovery context.
2. Users understand likely consequences before committing to actions.
3. Recovery guidance is available when rollback is not one-click.
notes: This task should work with advanced branch ops, not bolt on after the fact.

comments.author: NamPT
comments.text: Reasoning note: “undo everything” is aspirational, but the practical interpretation is still powerful: preview aggressively, expose recovery context, and make scary operations legible.

title: Operation preview layer
description: Design the preview mechanism that explains what risky actions are about to do before they run.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Operation preview layer is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-1w7 without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

description: Provide rollback or recovery guidance paths that reduce fear around high-impact operations even when one-click undo is impossible.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Undo and recovery affordances is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-1w7 without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: Enhanced Commit Graph UX. Extend the graph beyond MVP usefulness into a powerful large-history navigation tool with better search, filtering, and noise-reduction affordances. This task exists to move from baseline graph visibility to true power-user fluency.
design: This task deepens the graph from usable to expert-grade. The point is to improve navigation in dense histories through search, filtering, and noise reduction rather than only cosmetic enhancements.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Users can find relevant commits faster.
2. Dense histories become easier to interpret.
3. Improvements preserve performance expectations from the foundation.
notes: Treat large-history ergonomics as the success measure.

title: Graph search and filter controls
description: Add ways to find relevant commits and refs quickly inside large histories. This should improve retrieval speed without destabilizing graph performance.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Graph search and filter controls is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-1x9 without widening scope beyond the stated description.

title: Design System Tokens Baseline
description: Define and stabilize the visual token contract and core UI primitives needed to build mongit consistently. Scope includes semantic colors, spacing, typography, surface treatments, and foundational controls mapped to the product's 'premium calm' direction; it excludes a fully exhaustive component library.
design: This task defines the visual contract for the app. The point is not superficial polish alone; it is to prevent ad hoc styling and make future product surfaces cheaper and more coherent to build.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Semantic tokens exist for core colors, spacing, typography, and surfaces.
2. Tokens support the project's premium-calm direction.
3. Base primitives consume tokens rather than hard-coded values.
notes: Do not overbuild a full design system library here; establish the contract that future screens can extend.
close_reason: Epic complete: all 3 children closed (token contract bd-1zx.1, theme switching bd-1zx.2, UI primitives bd-1zx.3). 127 tokens in app.css, theme switching with FOUC prevention, 4 UI components consuming tokens exclusively. All gates green.

title: Changes Workspace and Partial Staging. Implement the local-changes workspace where users can inspect modified files and perform hunk-level and line-level staging without terminal patch mode. This is a core power-user requirement and one of the clearest value propositions of the app.
design: This task delivers one of the project's clearest value propositions: shaping commit contents visually without patch-mode friction. The workspace should make file-, hunk-, and line-level intent explicit.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Modified files are accurately represented.
2. Hunk staging/unstaging is reliable.
3. Line-level flows exist for precision work.
4. Errors are clear when patch application fails.
notes: This workflow should feel safer and more legible than raw git add -p.

comments.author: NamPT
comments.text: Reasoning note: partial staging is one of the strongest product hooks for the target persona. Preserve correctness and clarity over flashy interaction design; users will notice subtle staging mistakes immediately.

title: Changed files model and workspace list
description: Implement the data model and UI list for the local changes workspace so users can inspect changed files with accurate repository state.

title: Keyboard-First Command System. Implement the command palette and shortcut baseline so core workflows are accessible without mouse-heavy interaction. This task exists because keyboard-first behavior is a core product principle for the target user.
design: This task operationalizes the keyboard-first principle. The command palette and shortcuts should make mongit feel designed for terminal-native power users rather than retrofitted for them.
acceptance_criteria:
1. A command palette exists and is useful.
2. Core workflows have discoverable shortcuts.
3. Keyboard-only navigation is practical for common flows.
notes: This should span navigation, authoring, and investigation paths rather than becoming an isolated utility.

title: Command registry and palette UI
description: Implement the searchable command system that exposes major actions from a central keyboard-first entry point.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Command registry and palette UI is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-268 without widening scope beyond the stated description.

acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Core shortcut bindings is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-268 without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: AI Workflow Intelligence. Add bounded, assistive intelligence such as semantic conflict help, diff narration, and rebase guidance. This should remain additive and non-blocking, preserving user trust and the standalone-first philosophy.
design: This task introduces optional assistive intelligence to high-friction workflows like conflicts, diffs, and rebases. The design principle is bounded assistance: suggest, explain, and warn, but do not take control away from deterministic Git behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
1. AI features are optional and failure-tolerant.
2. Suggestions are contextual and clearly framed.
3. Core workflows remain functional without any AI path.
notes: Trust boundaries matter here more than novelty.

comments.author: NamPT
comments.text: Reasoning note: AI features should be assistive and bounded. Preserve deterministic workflows as the source of truth and present model output with humility rather than authority.

description: Create the bounded integration layer for optional model calls so assistive features remain failure-tolerant and well-contained.

title: Advanced Branch Operations
description: Implement merge, rebase, cherry-pick, and a guided interactive rebase baseline with appropriate previews and safeguards. This task is essential for the product thesis of JetBrains-grade depth in a standalone client.
design: This task expands branch operations from the current MVP baseline into professional workflows. Merge, rebase, cherry-pick, and guided interactive rebase need strong guardrails because they are powerful but high-risk.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Advanced operations exist with meaningful preflight checks.
2. Failure modes are understandable.
3. Interactive rebase has a usable guided surface.
notes: Build on the existing git resolver and command/error model instead of forking logic.

comments.author: NamPT
comments.text: Reasoning note: advanced branch operations are where user trust can be won or lost. Previews, guardrails, and error specificity matter as much as the happy path.

acceptance_criteria:
1. Core app flows run on Windows.
2. Runtime and packaging differences are handled explicitly.
3. Quality bar remains aligned with macOS expectations.
notes: Do not quietly introduce cross-platform compromises that weaken the core app.

title: MVP Branch Operations on Hybrid Git Engine
description: Production-facing branch operations for MVP: create, switch, delete (safe + force), fetch, pull, push (with --force-with-lease). Uses CLI write-path via GitResolver (depends on bd-2na). Origin-only remote scope for MVP. System git credential delegation. Structured error mapping for frontend consumption.
close_reason: Shipped: all 6 PRD tasks pass (BranchOpError enum, delete pre-flight, fetch/pull/push async, Tauri commands, failure-mode tests, full verification). 71/71 tests pass, 0 clippy warnings. Review fix applied for create/switch error routing.

comments.author: NamPT
comments.text: Created prd.md: score 91/100, 1 refinement round, 6 tasks, 7 success criteria

title: Stacked Diffs Workflow. Add a first-class model and UI for stacked changes so advanced users can manage dependent commits or branches more safely and visually than with raw Git commands alone.
design: This task adds a first-class model for dependent changes. The point is to make stacked workflows explicit, visible, and safer than raw branch juggling for advanced users.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Stacks have an explicit model.
2. Visuals and commands make dependencies understandable.
3. Editing a stack is safer than ad hoc manual commands.
notes: This should integrate with existing graph and branch-operation foundations.

title: Stack model and graph projection
description: Define the explicit data model for dependent changes and connect it to graph-friendly visuals.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Stack model and graph projection is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-2wk without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: Stack operations UI and command layer
description: Provide the commands and interface for editing stacks safely once the model exists.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Stack operations UI and command layer is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-2wk without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: Advanced Blame. Extend the blame experience with noise-reduction controls like hiding revisions and constraining historical ranges, so blame becomes more useful on real-world codebases with mechanical commits and refactors.
design: This task deepens blame usefulness by reducing noise from mechanical commits and large refactors. It should help users ask better historical questions without drowning in irrelevant attribution.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Noisy revisions can be hidden or excluded.
2. Historical range constraints are useful in practice.
3. Blame remains connected to broader history navigation.
notes: This builds directly on the history investigation baseline from V1.

title: Revision exclusion controls
description: Implement controls for excluding noisy revisions from blame output so users can focus on meaningful attribution.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Revision exclusion controls is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-31j without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: Blame range mode
description: Allow blame to be constrained to a useful historical window so large code histories remain tractable.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Blame range mode is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-31j without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: Conflict Resolution v1 with 3-Pane Baseline. Provide a practical in-app conflict workflow for common text conflicts. Scope includes conflict detection, entry into a resolver, basic ours/theirs/manual-edit actions, and completion flow; it does not yet include semantic or AI assistance.
design: This task provides the first in-app conflict workflow. The goal is not to solve every future merge challenge, but to make common text-conflict resolution practical enough that users do not have to immediately drop back to external tools.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Conflict files are discoverable.
2. Users can enter a 3-pane or equivalent resolver flow.
3. Ours/theirs/manual resolution paths are present.
4. Completing the resolution is understandable.
notes: Keep scope tight; semantic conflict help belongs to later phases.

comments.author: NamPT
comments.text: Reasoning note: keep the first version practical. It is better to resolve common text conflicts well than to promise a sophisticated universal merge experience too early.

title: Conflict detection and resolver entry flow
description: Detect unmerged/conflicted files and provide a clean path into the in-app resolver. The key deliverable is discoverability and flow continuity.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Conflict detection and resolver entry flow is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-3gr without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: Three-pane editor actions and resolution completion
description: Provide the basic resolution actions and completion flow needed to resolve common text conflicts in-app.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Three-pane editor actions and resolution completion is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-3gr without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: Repo Home and Navigation Shell
description: Create mongit's practical entry flow so a user can open a repo, return to recent repos, and immediately understand the current state. This task is the shell around all subsequent workflows and should establish predictable navigation and state persistence.
design: This task creates the entry flow and navigation shell around all later workflows. A user should be able to re-open mongit, pick a repository, and understand the current state without hunting for context.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Local repo open flow is practical.
2. Recent repos are persisted and usable.
3. The shell makes core state discoverable immediately.
notes: The shell should feel like a product surface, not a spike demo launcher.

title: Repo selection and persistence
description: Implement the workflow for opening a local repository and persisting recent repos for return visits. This establishes mongit's real entry point as a product.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Repo selection and persistence is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-6ew without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: Home state summary widgets
description: Add the summary surfaces that show a repo's current branch and actionable state immediately on load. The goal is orientation, not dashboard bloat.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Home state summary widgets is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-6ew without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: MVP Core Git Client
description: Deliver the first shippable daily-driver workflow for solo power developers. This epic should cover repo home/navigation, graph productization, local changes workspace with partial staging, commit authoring and sync, baseline conflict resolution, keyboard-first flows, and packaging so mongit becomes practically usable beyond spikes.
design: Purpose: deliver the first shippable daily-driver version of mongit for solo power developers. This epic should turn the existing backend strength and spike surfaces into a coherent end-user workflow. Future agents should optimize for end-to-end usability rather than isolated technical wins.
acceptance_criteria:
1. User can open a repo and navigate core state confidently.
2. User can inspect history, curate local changes, and create/push commits.
3. Common conflicts are resolvable inside the app.
4. Core workflows are accessible from keyboard-first entry points.
5. MVP can be packaged and tested outside the author machine.
notes: Leverage the existing git resolver, branch operations backend, commit graph spike, and watcher work rather than rebuilding those concerns.

comments.author: NamPT
comments.text: Background for future self: this epic is the first point where mongit stops being a strong technical foundation and starts becoming a coherent product. When making tradeoffs, prefer end-to-end usability over extra platform cleverness. A smaller but actually shippable vertical slice beats another isolated spike.

title: V1.1 Differentiation Power-Ups: add features that make mongit meaningfully better than mainstream Git GUIs for solo power developers, including work buckets, shelf, advanced blame, pre-commit checks, branch-scoped workspace context, and scalable log search.
design: Purpose: add differentiators that make mongit better than mainstream Git GUIs rather than merely competitive. This phase should focus on context-management, workflow acceleration, and expert-quality ergonomics for solo developers.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Users can organize work more flexibly than with raw staging alone.
2. Historical navigation and context restoration become materially faster.
3. Pre-commit quality and blame workflows are meaningfully stronger than baseline clients.
notes: These items should not block MVP/V1 closure; they are strategic upgrades once the core workflow is trustworthy.

comments.author: NamPT
comments.text: Background for future self: this phase should make mongit feel distinct, not just complete. Focus on features that reduce mental load, improve context switching, and let a solo power user organize work better than standard Git GUIs allow.

title: Commit Authoring and Sync. Add the workflow that turns curated staged changes into clean local history and synchronized remote state. Scope includes commit/amend behavior, push/pull UX integration, and clear user feedback around success and failure.
design: This task turns staged intent into durable history. It should connect commit creation, amend, and remote synchronization into one coherent authoring flow built on the already-implemented backend git plumbing.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Commit and amend behaviors are available and clear.
2. Push/pull integration exposes useful feedback.
3. Users can go from staged work to synchronized branch state without leaving the app.
notes: Reuse the existing branch-operation backend wherever possible.

comments.author: NamPT
comments.text: Reasoning note: the backend already has strong Git plumbing for branch/sync behavior. This task should turn that capability into a humane authoring flow rather than duplicating logic or inventing parallel command paths.

title: Commit and amend command pipeline
description: Implement the authoring path that validates staged intent, creates commits, and supports amend without confusing the user about repository state.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Commit and amend command pipeline is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-9a4 without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: Push and pull UX integration
description: Surface remote sync operations through the UI with clear feedback, progress context, and typed failure handling where possible.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Push and pull UX integration is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-9a4 without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: Workspace Context per Branch and Log Index. Preserve useful UI context by branch and add scalable history search so returning to work and finding past changes both become dramatically faster.
design: This task improves return-to-work speed and large-history retrieval by preserving branch-scoped UI context and adding scalable log search. It is about reducing cognitive setup time for power users.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Branch-scoped workspace context restores predictably.
2. History search scales to large repositories.
3. Search and restored context actually save user time.
notes: The pairing matters: context restoration and log search should reinforce each other.

title: Branch-scoped workspace state persistence
description: Persist and restore useful UI context by branch so returning to work has less cognitive setup cost.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Branch-scoped workspace state persistence is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-d4a without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: Commit log indexing and search backend
description: Add scalable history indexing and retrieval so users can find relevant past changes quickly in large repos.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Commit log indexing and search backend is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-d4a without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: Optional Cloud Features (Sync and Backup). Add opt-in cloud-backed convenience features such as settings or workspace backup without compromising the project's standalone-first identity or forcing accounts.
design: This task adds opt-in cloud convenience without turning mongit into an account-first product. The cloud layer should be additive, transparent, and respectful of local-only usage.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Sync/backup is clearly optional.
2. Data scope is legible to users.
3. Local-only behavior remains first-class.
notes: Guard the standalone-first philosophy here.

title: Opt-in sync architecture
description: Define the optional cloud architecture so users can understand and control what is synchronized.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Opt-in sync architecture is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-ef4 without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: Backup and restore UX with privacy controls
description: Create the user-facing backup and restore flows with explicit privacy and scope controls.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Backup and restore UX with privacy controls is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-ef4 without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: Foundation Reliability and Developer Guardrails
description: Add the reliability checks, smoke-test flows, and baseline failure handling needed so future agents can build on the graph/diff/watcher foundation safely. This task should capture what must stay true, how to verify it quickly, and where the sharp edges are.
design: This task captures how the foundation should be verified and where it is fragile. The main value is future-agent leverage: make it cheap to detect regressions in graph, diff, and watcher behavior before building more features on top.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Smoke checks exist for the most important foundation flows.
2. Failure modes degrade gracefully.
3. Future agents can quickly learn what must be preserved.
notes: This task depends on graph and diff/watcher work being real enough to validate.

title: Graph and diff smoke-test workflows
description: Define quick verification flows that prove the core graph and diff foundations still work after future changes. These checks should be cheap enough that agents will actually run them.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Graph and diff smoke-test workflows is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-egw without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: Failure-mode handling baseline
description: Codify graceful handling for missing repos, malformed state, watcher interruption, and similar foundation-level failures. The goal is to prevent brittle spikes from masquerading as platform capability.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Failure-mode handling baseline is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-egw without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: Spike D: Diff Viewer and File Watching Backbone. Establish the reusable foundation for showing repository changes and reacting to file-system updates. Scope includes CodeMirror diff integration, watcher lifecycle and debounce behavior, Tauri event bridging, and frontend refresh plumbing. This task exists because staging and conflict workflows depend on trustworthy diff and watcher infrastructure.
design: This task establishes the backbone for repository-change awareness. The diff surface and watcher/event path must be trustworthy because staging, conflict resolution, and ambient status all depend on them.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Diff component renders repository change data reliably.
2. Watcher lifecycle is stable and debounced correctly.
3. Frontend refresh behavior is scoped and predictable.
4. File changes do not require blunt full-app reload patterns.
notes: Treat watcher correctness and lifecycle management as first-order concerns, not incidental plumbing.

comments.author: NamPT
comments.text: Reasoning note: watcher correctness is product correctness. If file changes are missed or surfaced inconsistently, staging/conflict UI will feel untrustworthy no matter how polished the components look.

comments.author: NamPT
comments.text: Refined: draft → prd.md (score: 93/100, 1 round)

title: CodeMirror diff integration shell
description: Create the stable diff-view shell that can render repository changes and recover gracefully from loading or parsing issues. This subtask exists to establish a reusable diff surface for later staging and conflict work.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: CodeMirror diff integration shell is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-htm without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: Rust watcher service and debounce lifecycle
description: Implement or harden the watcher service lifecycle, including state ownership, debounce behavior, and normalized event emission. The goal is a watcher that is trustworthy under real editing activity.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Rust watcher service and debounce lifecycle is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-htm without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: Tauri event bridge and targeted UI refresh path
description: Connect backend watcher events into frontend state so only the affected surfaces refresh when repository changes occur. This should avoid full-app invalidation patterns and make change propagation understandable.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Tauri event bridge and targeted UI refresh path is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-htm without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: Git engine hybrid
description: Validate git2 read-path coverage, command invocation strategy for write-path, repo access pattern decision: open-per-call vs Arc<Mutex<…>>
close_reason: Implemented git engine hybrid: GitError enum, Git2Repository (status/diff/log/branches), GitCli (create/switch branch), refactored commands.rs, validated open-per-call pattern. 13 tests, clippy clean, pnpm check clean.

comments.author: NamPT
comments.text: Created prd.md: score 90/100, 3 refinement rounds, 6 tasks, 9 success criteria

title: Stash and Tag Management
description: Add common Git operations that power users expect to complete inside the client rather than bouncing back to the terminal. Scope includes stash lifecycle operations and tag browsing/creation flows.
design: This task closes common Git-operation gaps that otherwise keep users tethered to the terminal. Stash and tag support should be practical, explicit, and reversible.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Stash lifecycle operations are visible and usable.
2. Tags can be browsed and managed.
3. These workflows integrate with the broader navigation shell.
notes: Keep the UX clear about what is transient (stash) versus durable (tags).

title: Stash operations UI and command flow
description: Implement stash creation, inspection, apply/pop, and drop workflows that are explicit and understandable.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Stash operations UI and command flow is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-zc5 without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.

title: Tag browser and tag operations
description: Add browsing and management for tags so release markers become visible and manageable in-app.
acceptance_criteria: Completion intent: Tag browser and tag operations is implemented in a way that advances parent bead bd-zc5 without widening scope beyond the stated description.
notes: Child bead generated during hierarchy decomposition. Treat this as an implementation-sized slice of its parent, not an invitation to redesign adjacent areas.
